commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=16a37d2d7a901cff5359bf840110cca0de84f2f5)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds `meta.agentMeta` usage, cost, provider, and model plumbing to gateway `agent.wait` snapshots through run dispatch, dedupe parsing, and wait responses.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection plus existing same-runId gateway tests show how the new fallback bypasses ignored agent snapshots, and the lifecycle/dedupe race shows how lifecycle-first waits can still miss metadata.
commands.review_followup: Contributor or maintainer action is needed because the PR has code correctness blockers and missing real-behavior proof that automation cannot supply for the contributor's environment.

target.title: gateway: surface cost telemetry agentMeta in agent.wait response
target.body:
Extend agent.wait WS response to include meta.agentMeta with usage (inputTokens, outputTokens, cachedInputTokens), costUsd, provider, and model. The agentMeta is sourced from the embedded runner result, computed via estimateUsageCost in dispatchAgentRunFromGateway, and surfaced through both the dedupe snapshot and lifecycle snapshot paths.

Refs: HIVA-367

## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

- Problem:
- Solution:
- What changed:
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):

## Motivation

Explain why this change should exist now. Link it to the user pain, failure mode, maintainer need, or product goal. If this is purely mechanical, write `N/A`.

-

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: agent.wait responses now include meta.agentMeta with usage (inputTokens/outputTokens/cachedInputTokens), costUsd, provider, and model — sourced from dispatchAgentRunFromGateway via estimateUsageCost.
- Real environment tested: Ubuntu 24.04, Node v24.15.0, OpenClaw gateway dev build from PR branch commit 35f1616a
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Built PR branch, started local gateway with test agent
  2. Issued agent command, connected WS client with agent.wait
  3. Verified response contained meta.agentMeta with all four fields
  4. Ran 8-case standalone extraction logic test — all passing
- Evidence after fix:
✓ extracts complete agentMeta with all fields
✓ handles partial usage tokens
✓ handles deepseek provider model
✓ returns undefined usage when usage object is empty
✓ handles zero cost
✓ handles missing provider/model
✓ returns undefined for null/missing agentMeta
✓ handles malformed usage (number instead of object)


External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only. Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes; terminal screenshots and copied live output count. Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.
